weekly_log.vertical: B2B email & SMS marketing SaaS platforms
weekly_log.notes: Pushed all 9 Hot/Warm surfaced prospects from run 1 + run 2 into HubSpot after explicit approval: 9 Company records (each with a Note containing the full radar score breakdown + drafted, unsent opener) and 5 Contact records for named decision-makers (Birdsview's 2 co-founders, TxtCart's Kyle Bigley, SlickText's Matt Baglia, Customer.io's Colin Nederkoorn). No emails set on any contact (none verified). Company HubSpot IDs recorded per-entry above in `screened`.

Per Constantin's explicit 'rotate as much as possible in the beginning' instruction, moved to the next vertical in rotation rather than continuing to deep-mine vertical 1. Sourced 9 candidates: Rebuy Engine (63, Warm), Voyado/Nosto (55, Warm — deprioritize-leaning, already independently cited), Glood.AI (66, Warm), Maestra (58, Warm), RevenueHunt (59, Warm), Kickflip (65, Warm), PureClarity (58, Warm), AiTrillion (64, Warm), and LimeSpot (knockout — acquired by Pantastic Aug 2022). No company scored Hot in this batch — real data, not forced to hit a target tier. Same Nimble geo/include_answer 403 limitation — focus=general listicle-presence proxy used throughout. All 9 pushed to HubSpot with the surfaced 4 (Rebuy, Glood.AI, Kickflip, AiTrillion) getting pending DRAFT-NOT-SENT outreach Tasks.

Sourced and verified 6 companies this run: Viewlocked (85, Hot — fresh press, zero AI-citation footprint), WeRoad (74, Hot — fresh $58M Series C + US expansion, absent from comparisons), Layla (74, Hot — inconsistent AI-trip-planner positioning despite real traction), Wonderplan (69, Warm), plus 2 knockouts (GuideGeek — captive-parent/media ownership; TravelPerk — too large/household-name). Data-integrity note: the original research pass for this vertical screened additional Watch-tier candidates beyond these 6, but the underlying detail for those did not survive a context-window compaction mid-session — rather than fabricate names/scores to backfill them, only the fully-verified 6 (4 surfaced + 2 knockouts) were recorded and pushed to HubSpot. A future run can re-mine this vertical's long tail if a fuller count is wanted. Same Nimble 403 limitation/listicle-proxy method as every prior run.

Sourced and verified 6 companies this run: Antidote (84, Hot — fresh $5M seed + US expansion, zero AI-citation footprint), CARET/Caret Legal (63, Warm — real scale, absent from comparisons), Financial Cents (61, Warm), Jetpack Workflow (60, Warm), plus 2 knockouts (MyCase and CASEPeer — both owned by AffiniPay, an active acquirer; MyCase's exact independence has some confidence uncertainty, flagged as a judgment call not a hard fact). Same data-integrity note as the Travel vertical run above — only the fully-verified subset was recorded, no fabricated fillers. Same Nimble 403 limitation/listicle-proxy method as every prior run.

Pushed all 21 newly-sourced companies from the E-commerce/Travel/Professional-services rotation runs into HubSpot (9+6+6, including 5 knockouts pushed for record-keeping per the 'push all entries' precedent). Created 17 pending 'DRAFT — NOT SENT' outreach Tasks (Type: Email, Status: Not Started) associated to their Company records, covering all 12 newly-surfaced Hot/Warm prospects across the 3 new verticals PLUS 5 retroactive openers for Phase-0 carryover prospects that were already in HubSpot but never had a drafted opener (EmailOctopus, Sender, MailerLite, Drip, Omnisend). Deliberately left `ai_visibility_score` blank on every entry — that field is reserved for a real Component A per-domain audit score, and none has actually been run against these domains; only the manual listicle-proxy `prospect_score` exists. No outreach was sent — every task is a draft pending Constantin's own review and approval, per his explicit instruction not to start sending emails yet.

Constantin ran a status check (44 screened, 26 pending tasks confirmed live in HubSpot) and gave 4 directives: (1) approve all 26 pending drafts — every task's subject/body relabeled from 'DRAFT — NOT SENT' to 'APPROVED — READY TO SEND', still not actually sent (no send capability used; sending remains Constantin's own manual action from his inbox). (2) Create the missing Maropost task — found via get_crm_objects that Maropost (82, Hot) was already a HubSpot company from an earlier pre-compaction batch but had no opener/task; drafted one grounded in its already-stored top_gap ('Invisible in AI best email tool answers despite $10M raised / 377 employees, repositioned to unified commerce'), found its CEO (Ross Andrew Paquette, Founder/Chairman & CEO) via LinkedIn, created a HIGH-priority pending task (id 502979236042), and added maropost.com's hubspot_company_id (436797303012) to `screened` above — it had been missing despite the company existing in HubSpot since the original Task #8 batch. (3) Automation stays off until this first round of manual runs is complete — confirmed, nothing scheduled. (4) LinkedIn research pass for every task still showing a '[Name]' placeholder: searched 18 companies total (17 flagged earlier + Vero, missed in the original count) and found a named decision-maker for 16 of them — Jetpack Workflow (David Cristello), Financial Cents (Shahram Zarshenas), WeRoad (Fabio Bin), Rebuy Engine (John Erck), AiTrillion (Sachin Dhanotiya), Kickflip (Simon Vallieres), Omnisend (Rytis Lauris), Drip (Mogens Møller), MailerLite (Indre Sizovaite), Sender (Edgaras Vaitkevičius), EmailOctopus (Gareth Bull), Postscript (Adam Turner), SendPulse (Kostyantyn Makarov), Vero (Chris Hexton), GetResponse (Simon Grabowski), Salesmsg (Chris Brisson), plus Maropost (Ross Andrew Paquette, item 2 above). Also fixed 2 tasks that had a name in the original research writeup but were pushed with a generic [Name] placeholder anyway: Birdsview (Tim Kuesters) and Customer.io (Colin Nederkoorn). CARET (Caret Legal) and Glood.AI returned no individual decision-maker in LinkedIn search — both explicitly flagged in their HubSpot task subject/body ('⚠️ NO NAME FOUND') for Constantin to find and take over, per his instruction. Net: of 27 total pending tasks (26 approved + 1 new Maropost), 25 now have a named, personalized greeting; 2 are flagged for Constantin. Still nothing sent — every task remains a draft pending Constantin's own send.

Per Constantin's explicit 'yes, we need that asap' directive, ran BrandGEO's real production Instant Audit Engine (Component A — audit-domain.js / get-audit-report.js, screening depth, Gemini + Perplexity, 4 buyer-intent prompts, internal key) against all 31 previously-pushed prospect domains, replacing the manual listicle-presence proxy with genuine engine-derived AI Visibility Scores, dimension breakdowns, and citation evidence. Every domain returned status:ready, zero errors/timeouts/rate-limit blocks; total monthly prospecting spend well within the €200 budget cap.

CRITICAL FINDING #1 — a real product bug, not a sales problem: Component A synthesizes a brand's alias from the domain root alone (e.g. rebuyengine.com → 'rebuyengine'), which fails to match the real brand name whenever the two diverge meaningfully. Manually verified 4 domains against raw AI response text and confirmed all 4 were false zeros: Salesmsg (salesmessage.com, named 7/7 checks, consistently ranked #2), Kickflip (gokickflip.com, named 4/4, ranked #3-8), CARET Legal (caretlegal.com, named once, ranked #6/9), and Rebuy (rebuyengine.com, named twice, ranked #8-9/10). All 4 corrected in HubSpot with accurate, evidence-based pitches instead of a false 'you're invisible' claim.

CRITICAL FINDING #2 — 4 companies already have strong, consistent real AI visibility that directly contradicts the 'you're invisible' pitch angle: Customer.io (8/8 checks, 100%), EmailOctopus (5/8, once #1), Postscript (3/5, twice #1), MailerLite (4/7, once #1). All 4 downgraded to LOW priority in HubSpot, subject-prefixed '⚠️ RECONSIDER', with the original pitch flagged as inaccurate and a monitoring/defend-your-position reframe suggested instead — sending the original pitch to any of these would have been an easily-checkable false claim.

All 27 HubSpot outreach tasks updated with real audit evidence: 14 confirmed-clean genuine zeros (no change needed beyond a confirmation note), 5 corrections where the real data was more nuanced than the original pitch claimed (SendPulse, TxtCart, Wonderplan, Financial Cents, Drip), 4 alias-bug corrections (full pitch rewrite), and 4 reconsider-flags (priority downgraded, do-not-send-as-is). Zero HubSpot update failures across 3 batches (27/27 updated).

Four companies with real audit data but no HubSpot task yet: voyado.com (confirmed 0, no task drafted), revenuehunt.com (confirmed 0, no task drafted), pureclarity.com (confirmed 0, no task drafted), maestra.io (real score 76 — genuinely visible, NOT a good 'invisible' prospect, no task drafted). Awaiting Constantin's decision on whether to draft tasks for the 3 confirmed-zero companies and whether/how to pursue maestra.io with a different angle.

Still nothing sent — every task remains a draft pending Constantin's own manual send from his inbox. No automation/scheduling was set up. This session also surfaced a real, previously-undocumented limitation in audit-domain.js's brand-alias synthesis worth flagging to Master-DashboardDesign or Master-Reasoning as a product fix (affects any future anonymous domain audit, not just this prospecting run).

Per Constantin's directive ('we have component A, lets use it'), resolved the standing Nimble geo/include_answer 403 blocker by rewriting sales-radar/RADAR-RUN-PROMPT.md's Step 3 AI-visibility-gap signal to call Component A's audit-domain endpoint directly (curl + X-Internal-Key from the brandgeo_internal_audit_key memory), converting the real 0-100 ai_score into the existing 40-point signal via points = round(40 * (100 - ai_score) / 100). Verified live with a real curl call against getresponse.com before committing to the change. CLAUDE.md §10 updated with a RESOLVED note. Nimble upgrade explicitly rejected as unnecessary — Component A is free (server-side budget-capped already via _prospect_guard.js), more accurate (real engine responses vs a search-result proxy), and already built.

Then closed out the 4 flagged companies from the prior run: presented Constantin a full proposed-changes table (task subject/body/priority/decision-maker for all 4) and got explicit 'yes approve and include maestra.io'. Created 4 new 'DRAFT — NOT SENT' HubSpot Tasks (Type: Email, Status: Not Started), all successfully associated to their Company records: Voyado (id 504012685502, HIGH priority, pitch to Erica Sandelin Ekelund), RevenueHunt (id 503991046351, MEDIUM, pitch to Angel Diaz Ibarra with LinkedIn caveat), PureClarity (id 503997641971, MEDIUM, decision-maker explicitly left unverified in the task body rather than risk a fabricated/wrong name — two conflicting web-search results, neither reliably sourced), and Maestra (id 504089136333, MEDIUM, deliberately a monitoring/defense pitch rather than a visibility-gap pitch, since the real score is 76/100 — pitch to Alex Gornik). radar-state.json updated with hubspot_task_id for all 4. Scheduled/unattended automation remains explicitly off — still a manual, chat-driven process. Reachability (the weakest signal per the 2026-07-09 run's own note) was not specifically re-strengthened this session.

Per Constantin's explicit instruction ('set up a clay project where you collect the work email and make sure you have it already in the draft saved'), used the connected Clay MCP to look up work email addresses for the 4 decision-makers named in the prior run's HubSpot tasks, then created real Gmail drafts (not just HubSpot task text) for the ones with a verified address, per his stated preference that drafts live in Gmail for easy manual takeover.

Clay search-contacts-by-name found Erica Sandelin Ekelund (Voyado) and Alex Gornik (Maestra) directly; RevenueHunt's 'Angel Diaz Ibarra' returned notFoundContacts by that full name, so search-contacts (role-based: Founder/CEO/Co-Founder at revenuehunt.com) was used instead and found exactly one match, LinkedIn-masked as initials 'A.D.' (consistent with, not full confirmation of, the name). PureClarity's role-based search (CEO/Founder/Managing Director/Director) returned zero contacts, independently corroborating the prior run's 'no verified decision-maker' finding.

Email enrichment (Clay add-contact-data-points, type Email, polled via get-task-context): erica.sandelin@voyado.com (completed) and alex@maestra.io (completed) both resolved cleanly. RevenueHunt's 'A.D.' enrichment completed with 'No results found.' PureClarity had no contact to enrich at all.

Real Gmail drafts created for the 2 verified contacts via the connected Gmail MCP (create_draft): Voyado (draft id r-6621224879786001423, to erica.sandelin@voyado.com) and Maestra (draft id r-1385101561050047430, to alex@maestra.io) — both are genuine, addressed, ready-to-send drafts sitting in the Gmail account's Drafts folder, adapted from the HubSpot pitch text into proper outbound email format (no internal task framing, no em dashes). Per the standing anti-fabrication rule, no Gmail draft was created for RevenueHunt or PureClarity since Gmail's create_draft tool requires at least one real recipient and none could be verified — guessing an address was deliberately avoided. Their pitch text remains only in the HubSpot task body, exactly as before, flagged for Constantin's manual email lookup. One data point surfaced for transparency, not treated as disqualifying: Clay's LinkedIn-derived record for Erica Sandelin Ekelund lists her most recent listed role as Board Member at Papirfly (papirfly.com, since 2024-09) rather than her Voyado CEO title — the independently-resolved voyado.com email is what keeps this targeting sound despite that.

radar-state.json updated with gmail_draft_id and decision_maker_email fields on all 4 entries (null where not found), plus a fallback_general_inbox note on PureClarity (sales@pureclarity.com, publicly listed on their own site, not used automatically). Nothing was sent — every draft and task remains pending Constantin's own manual review and send.

weekly_log.vertical: voyado.com + maestra.io (send confirmation + HubSpot deals + LinkedIn fallback)
weekly_log.notes:
Constantin confirmed he sent both the Voyado and Maestra Gmail drafts (created last run) after manually moving them from Browser 2 (Workfully) to Browser 1 (CST) — the drafts were created under the wrong browser/account despite the standing [[brandgeo_correct_browser]] rule, a recurring mistake now reinforced in that memory to explicitly cover Gmail draft creation, not just Claude-in-Chrome navigation. radar-state.json marked both as email_sent:true, email_sent_date 2026-07-16.
